description: §65.2.2 — a state rule (`:hover`) wins WHILE its condition holds; that is a deterministic LAYER over the base, not a conflict. `.btn { color: red }` + `.btn:hover { color: blue }` on one element is the 95% case (base + hover) and fires NEITHER E-STYLE-CONFLICT nor W-STYLE-CONFLICT-POSSIBLE. The error fires exactly where a human would be surprised (an unconditional both-match), and nowhere else.
rationale: §65.2.2: a `:hover`/`:focus`/`[attr]`/`@media`/`@container` rule is a deterministic layer over the base while its condition holds — well-defined, local, predictable — NOT a conflict. This carve-out (base + conditional) is what keeps the guarantee affordable; it never fires an error.
